results.title: Death Threats Target Colombian Midfielder After World Cup Exit
results.description: After Colombia's elimination from the FIFA World Cup 2026, midfielder Jaminton Campaz and his family received alarming death threats. The Colombian football federation condemned this behavior and called for respect in sports. Campaz expressed his frustration on social media, urging fans to prioritize respect over hatred. This incident recalls the tragic history of violence in Colombian football, notably the murder of defender Andrés Escobar after a critical mistake in the 1994 World Cup. As Switzerland moves forward in the tournament, the federation emphasizes the need for unity and respect in the sport.

results.title: AI Music Training Hits Two Courts in July: Suno Faces Verdicts in Munich and Boston
results.description: Suno copyright lawsuit arrives in two courts this July: Germany's Munich Regional Court delivers the GEMA v. Suno verdict July 31 while Massachusetts holds the first US hearing on AI music training fair use. Audio fingerprinting found 61,000 copyrighted songs in Suno's training data, and major-label AI licensing deals give courts a market to weigh against fair use.
